folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 10 - Core Building
player_summary: A decaying artists’ co-op in Manhattan where Abigail Wright vanished.
gm_truth: By day it looks merely strange; by night it opens into the Night Floors and the tenants live double lives.
subareas: Front entry; halls; stairs; basement; tenant doors; top-floor stair landing; roof-door threshold
linked_npcs: Abigail Wright; Thomas Manuel; Roger Carun; Michelle Vanfitz; Louis Post; Cynthia Lechance; Giuradanda; David Langford
linked_clues: unpaid utilities; ceased shopping; last outgoing phone call; Wright case timeline

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 40 - External Investigation Sites
player_summary: The nonprofit office that owns and manages the building.
gm_truth: Gives the clean day-world truth: tenancy, late rent, and ARTLIFE’s quiet desire to empty the building for renovation.
linked_clues: ownership records; rent delinquency; eviction pressure

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 10 - Core Building
player_summary: The missing artist’s apartment, transformed into a baffling shrine-wall of papers and objects.
gm_truth: Central clue location; prolonged exposure affects Agents; the apartment is the clearest physical sign that the building is already infected.
subareas: Main room / shrine wall; stripped floor; seized electronics traces
linked_npcs: Abigail Wright; Thomas Wright; Giuradanda; Thomas Manuel
linked_clues: shrine strata; occult seal; receipt; sheet music; mysterious play pages; device remnants

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 20 - Tenant Apartments
player_summary: The apartment of the young painter across from Wright’s door.
gm_truth: Contains clear signs of surveillance, fixation, and night-side leakage even when Manuel denies anything is wrong.
subareas: Main room; hidden recess in plant base; refrigerator; door threshold facing Wright’s apartment
linked_clues: Ambrose locket; cassette recorder; hidden microphone wire; impossible food dates

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 30 - Building Subareas
player_summary: A hidden workroom in the building basement used for painting.
gm_truth: Holds “My Great Work,” linseed oil for “Sami,” and paintings that later echo real-world horrors.
subareas: Small studio room; stacked canvases; linseed oil pails
linked_clues: “My Great Work”; linseed oil; ominous paintings

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 20 - Tenant Apartments
player_summary: The cluttered apartment of a genre writer who has stopped showing up for his life.
gm_truth: Carun is altering or channeling material tied to the play; his apartment supports the last-call clue path and paranoia signs.
subareas: Living space / writing area; openings with telltale hairs; word processor area
linked_clues: 19 APR call; answering-machine lead; word processor text; hairs on doors/openings

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 20 - Tenant Apartments
player_summary: A cramped writer’s apartment buried in books.
gm_truth: By day it contains Wright’s battered Red Book; by night an archway opens to endless book-rooms of the Night Floors.
subareas: Front room; bedroom nook; bathroom; kitchenette; living room; night archway; endless book-rooms
linked_clues: Red Book; endless books; impossible food dates; mace / tomahawk threat; feminist reading of the play

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 20 - Tenant Apartments
player_summary: The workspace of a talented illustrator whose life has come apart.
gm_truth: By night he becomes far more dangerous; the apartment is more a pressure-cooker than a clue cache.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 10 - Core Building
player_summary: The upper landing where the building stops behaving correctly.
gm_truth: Langford vanished here while working the cable box at the day/night threshold. This is also tied to Wright’s 1994 mugging near the roof door.
subareas: Landing; cable box; roof access threshold
linked_clues: Langford disappearance; cable junction; roof-door anomaly

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 50 - Night Floors
player_summary: Endless rooms beyond the roof exit where the building no longer obeys reality.
gm_truth: The lost and the mad live here as dark caricatures; Wright cannot be recovered here in Part One, only understood.
subareas: Endless rooms; corridors; abandoned party rooms; tenant spillover spaces; access to specific resident encounters
linked_npcs: David Langford; Mark Roark; Henri de Calvados Castaigne; Encyclopedia Salesman; The Dog; Thomas Manuel; Michelle Vanfitz
linked_clues: Langford survival; Roark’s hotel logic; Castaigne’s claims; Dog manifestations; deeper invitations to continue downward

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 50 - Night Floors
player_summary: A night-side lounge that should not exist in the building.
gm_truth: Manuel places Wright’s going-away party here; it is a stable enough named anchor to warrant its own location entry.
linked_clues: going-away party; “moved on / moved upstairs” testimony
